column1: #   command: literal tool_input.command. The marker __NL__ expands to LF
#            (used by heredoc cases). Backslash sequences like \n are passed
#            through unchanged so printf "label: foo\n" stays literal.
#   env    : optional `KEY=VAL` env-var prefix (single segment, no spaces inside value)
# Lines beginning with `#` are skipped. Blank lines skipped. Tabs separate columns.
# Trailing column may be empty (no env).
# Regression for the gap where `rm -rf $HOME` (no subpath) was ALLOWED because
# HOME is whitelisted. Whitelist only certifies the var is shell-typed, not
# that the target is bounded — bare `$HOME` rms the user's entire home,
# and `$HOME/` rms `/` if HOME is somehow unset (Valve/steam-for-linux#3671:
# Real-world FPs from /cso 2026-04-30 (4 reproductions) — see CHANGELOG v0.5.5.

column3: cat <<EOF__NL__some prose mentioning npx pkg here__NL__EOF

column2: heredoc with quoted tag + npx in body

column1: # === indirect-call detection (default-ON since v0.21.8; was opt-in v0.6.0-v0.21.7 via BASH_SAFETY_INDIRECT_CALL=1) ===
# === indirect-call opt-out (BASH_SAFETY_INDIRECT_CALL=0 disables unwrap; escape hatch for users who hit FP) ===
# === indirect-call FP guards (default-ON; bypass tokens / whitelists / pins survive unwrap) ===
# === multi-line bash: pattern extraction must not bleed across lines (v0.17.2 dogfood) ===
# operated per-line; lines without rm/npx passed through unchanged and either:
#   - false-ALLOW: rm-rf on line 2 → head -n1 returns line 1 (no rm content) →
#     rm_target empty → deny path never fires. §8 SAFETY bypass.
#   - false-DENY: npx-pin on line 2 → npx_tail starts with line 1's first token
#     (e.g. `TMP=$(mktemp`) → flagged as unpinned package. Innocent denied.
# Fix: SANITIZED_CMD_FLAT collapses `\n` → ` ` once, before both extraction passes.

column2: multi-line npx pinned on line 2 (was false-DENY)
multi-line npx local path on line 2
multi-line npx unpinned on line 2 (real catch)

column1: # === sanitize state-machine fix (silent §8 bypass via adjacent $-quoted regions) ===
# Pre-fix `sed -E 's/"[^"$]*"/""/g'` could pair the closing `"` of one
# $-containing string with the opening `"` of the next, eating `&& rm -rf`
# (or any other danger) between them. Repro: `echo "$A" && rm -rf "$B"`
# was sanitized to `echo "$A""$B"` and the rm-detector saw no `rm` at all.
# Fix: state-machine pairing in sanitize_cmd. The juxtaposition form
# `echo "$A" rm -rf "$B"` (no operator) is allowed by the readonly fast-path
# — bash-semantically it's just `echo` with 4 literal args, no rm execution.

column2: npx unpinned after && between two $-quoted strings
npx unpinned scoped after && between two $-quoted strings

column1: # Spec §8 SAFETY's "Validate the var inline" recommended form. The hook
# now recognizes a `${VARNAME:?...}` set-or-exit guard whose varname
# matches the rm-rf target, mirroring the literal form printed in the
# deny message. Other guard forms ([[ -n ]], `set -u`, control flow) are

column2: :? guard for wrong var (SAFE guarded, EVIL deleted)
[[ -n ]] non-empty check is not a recognized guard
backslash-escaped literal guard in echo is not real

column1: # === v0.21.4: rm-flag coverage extension + per-segment iteration ===
# Pre-fix `RM_FLAG_REGEX` required `rm` followed by a SINGLE `-*[rRfF]*` flag
# (`rm -v -i -rf`) were FN. Also `.*${RM_FLAG_REGEX}//` was greedy, anchored
# at the LAST rm-rf in a multi-rm chain — earlier unguarded rm-rfs were
# silently skipped. Fix: per-segment iteration (split on `;`/`&&`/`||`/`|`/`&`/

column2: rm --recursive --help (long flag w/ help, no var)
